task.description: Compute min, max, sum of a number array
task.prompt: Given these numbers: 42 17 93 8 55 71 3 88 29 64. Write a bash script that computes the minimum, maximum, and sum. Print exactly three lines: 'min: N', 'max: N', 'sum: N'.

content.text: Given these numbers: 42 17 93 8 55 71 3 88 29 64. Write a bash script that computes the minimum, maximum, and sum. Print exactly three lines: 'min: N', 'max: N', 'sum: N'.

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash

nums=(42 17 93 8 55 71 3 88 29 64)

min=${nums[0]}
max=${nums[0]}
sum=0

for n in "${nums[@]}"; do
  (( n < min )) && min=$n
  (( n > max )) && max=$n
  (( sum += n ))
done

printf "min: %d\n" "$min"
printf "max: %d\n" "$max"
printf "sum: %d\n" "$sum"
```

task.description: Build and demonstrate a CLI TODO app
task.prompt: Build a command-line TODO app. Create /app/todo.sh that supports these subcommands: 'add <task>' appends a task to /app/tasks.txt, 'list' prints all tasks with line numbers, 'done <number>' removes that line from the file. Then demonstrate: add 'Buy groceries', add 'Write tests', add 'Deploy app', list all tasks, mark task 1 as done, list again to show remaining tasks.

content.text: Build a command-line TODO app. Create /app/todo.sh that supports these subcommands: 'add <task>' appends a task to /app/tasks.txt, 'list' prints all tasks with line numbers, 'done <number>' removes that line from the file. Then demonstrate: add 'Buy groceries', add 'Write tests', add 'Deploy app', list all tasks, mark task 1 as done, list again to show remaining tasks.

input.commands:
mkdir -p /app
cat > /app/todo.sh <<'EOF'
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

TASK_FILE="/app/tasks.txt"

usage() {
  cat <<'USAGE'
Usage:
  todo.sh add <task>
  todo.sh list
  todo.sh done <number>
USAGE
}

cmd=${1-}
case "$cmd" in
  add)
    shift || true
    if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then usage >&2; exit 1; fi
    task="$*"
    mkdir -p "$(dirname "$TASK_FILE")"
    printf '%s\n' "$task" >> "$TASK_FILE"
    ;;
  list)
    if [ -f "$TASK_FILE" ]; then
      nl -ba "$TASK_FILE"
    fi
    ;;
  done)
    shift || true
    if [ $# -ne 1 ]; then usage >&2; exit 1; fi
    n="$1"
    if ! [[ "$n" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] || [ "$n" -le 0 ]; then
      echo "Invalid task number: $n" >&2
      exit 1
    fi
    if [ ! -f "$TASK_FILE" ]; then
      echo "No tasks." >&2
      exit 1
    fi
    total=$(wc -l < "$TASK_FILE" | tr -d ' ')
    if [ "$n" -gt "$total" ]; then
      echo "Task number out of range." >&2
      exit 1

task.description: Generate table of contents from markdown headings
task.prompt: Read /doc/README.md and generate a table of contents from its headings (lines starting with # or ##). Insert the TOC after the first heading, formatted as a markdown list with '- [Heading Text](#anchor)' where anchor is the heading text lowercased with spaces replaced by hyphens. Write the result back to the file.

files./doc/README.md:
# Project Alpha

Introduction to the project.

## Installation

Run the installer.

## Usage

How to use it.

## API Reference

Endpoint documentation.

## Contributing

PR guidelines.

content.text: Read /doc/README.md and generate a table of contents from its headings (lines starting with # or ##). Insert the TOC after the first heading, formatted as a markdown list with '- [Heading Text](#anchor)' where anchor is the heading text lowercased with spaces replaced by hyphens. Write the result back to the file.

input.commands:
set -e
file=/doc/README.md
python3 - <<'PY'
import re, pathlib
path = pathlib.Path('/doc/README.md')
text = path.read_text(encoding='utf-8').splitlines()
# collect headings
headings=[]
for line in text:
    m = re.match(r'^(#{1,2})\s+(.*)$', line)
    if m:
        level=len(m.group(1))
        title=m.group(2).strip()
        # ignore empty
        if title:
            headings.append((level,title))

# first heading index
first_idx=None
for i,line in enumerate(text):
    if re.match(r'^#\s+.*', line):
        first_idx=i
        break
if first_idx is None:
    raise SystemExit('No top-level heading found')

# build toc from headings (excluding first heading?) Typically include all headings except the first title.
# Spec says generate from its headings lines starting with # or ##. Insert after first heading.
# We'll include all headings except the first one to avoid self-link.
first_heading_title=re.match(r'^#\s+(.*)$', text[first_idx]).group(1).strip()

def anchor(t):
    return re.sub(r'\s+','-', t.strip().lower())

toc_lines=[]
for level,title in headings:
    if level==1 and title==first_heading_title:
        continue
    indent='  '*(level-1)  # level1 no indent, level2 indent
    toc_lines.append(f"{indent}- [{title}](#{anchor(title)})")

# insert toc after first heading line, with blank line separation
out=[]
out.extend(text[:first_idx+1])
out.append('')
out.extend(toc_lines)
out.append('')
out.extend(text[first_idx+1:])
path.write_text('\n'.join(out)+"\n", encoding='utf-8')
PY
